heading: Honours that made history
lead: Hers is not simply a story of survival; it is a bold redefinition of what becomes possible when courage meets purpose.

items.title: Nari Shakti Puraskar
items.description: India's highest civilian honour for outstanding contribution to women's empowerment, conferred by the President of India.

items.title: Address at the United Nations, New York, USA
items.description: A motivational keynote at UN Headquarters that drew a standing ovation.

items.title: Honoured by Dr. A. P. J. Abdul Kalam
items.description: Personally recognised by the late President of India for topping Class 10 at the state level.

items.title: Took over the PM's social media
items.description: Chosen as a Woman Achiever to take over the Prime Minister's accounts on Women's Day.

items.title: Chair, WEF – India Economic Summit
items.description: Co-chaired alongside Union Cabinet Ministers and global business leaders.

items.title: Invited speaker at the World Bank
items.description: Addressed the International Day of Persons with Disabilities.

items.title: Showstopper, Fashion Week Chennai
items.description: Championing inclusive fashion on the runway.

items.title: 100 Change Agents & Newsmakers of the Decade
items.description: Named among the decade's most influential changemakers by Deccan Chronicle.

items.title: A PhD thesis, against all odds
items.description: Having lost both hands, she typed her entire doctoral thesis using a bone protruding from her stump — an extraordinary feat of determination.
